# Requirement: FR-006

- Title: Clear active-page indication in primary navigation
- Status: Active

## Requirement Statement

The system shall visibly indicate the currently active top-level page in the primary navigation across desktop and mobile breakpoints.

## Rationale

When all menu items look the same, visitors can lose their sense of place while moving between pages. A clear active state improves orientation, reduces friction, and makes the site feel more polished.

## Acceptance Conditions

- Home, Projects, and Contact links show a distinct active state on their corresponding pages.
- Active-page indication is implemented in the shared layout so behavior remains consistent across routes.
- The active state uses accessible semantics such as `aria-current="page"` and maintains clear visual contrast.
- Hover and keyboard focus styles remain usable and distinct from the active state.

## Success Metrics

- On each top-level route, exactly one primary navigation item is visibly highlighted and announced as the current page.
- Build, lint, and format checks remain green after the change.

## Non-Goals

- Breadcrumb navigation.
- Multi-level route grouping or section-aware side navigation.

## Constraints

- Preserve the static-first architecture and base-path-aware routing.
- Keep the implementation small, shared, and easy to maintain.
